item.title: Provider adapters call response.json() without try-catch — crashes on malformed API responses
item.intent: Provisioning must never crash with a raw JSON parse exception. All API response failures must surface as clean, actionable error messages with HTTP status and provider context.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/providers/digitalocean.ts (lines 100, 426, 469) and hetzner.ts (line 77) call response.json() without try-catch. If the cloud API returns non-JSON (HTML error page, gateway timeout, 5xx body), the unhandled exception propagates and crashes the entire provisioning flow with an opaque JSON parse error rather than a user-readable API error. Fix: wrap response.json() calls in try-catch, re-throw as a typed ProvisionError with the raw response status and body included.
acceptanceCriteria: No bare response.json() calls in any provider adapter without error handling
A malformed provider API response surfaces a readable ProvisionError with HTTP status, not a JSON parse crash
